author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm X

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Why, O Lord,” saith he, “hast Thou withdrawn afar off?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then he who thus inquired, as if all on a sudden he understood, or as if he asked, though he knew, that he might teach, adds, “Thou despisest in due seasons, in tribulations:” that is, Thou despisest seasonably, and causest tribulations to inflame men’s minds with longing for Thy coming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that fountain of life is sweeter to them that have much thirst.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he hints the reason of the delay, saying, “Whilst the ungodly vaunteth himself, the poor man is inflamed” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wondrous it is and true with what earnestness of good hope the little ones are inflamed unto an upright living by comparison with sinners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In which mystery it comes to pass, that even heresies are permitted to exist; not that heretics themselves wish this, but because Divine Providence worketh this result from their sins, which both maketh and ordaineth the light; but ordereth only the darkness, that by comparison therewith the light may be more pleasant, as by comparison with heretics the discovery of truth is more sweet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so, by this comparison, the approved, who are known to God, are made manifest among men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They are taken in their thoughts, which they think:” that is, their evil thoughts become chains to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how become they chains?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the sinner is praised,” saith he, “in the desires of his soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The tongues of flatterers bind souls in sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is pleasure in doing those things, in which not only is no reprover feared, but even an approver heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And he that does unrighteous deeds is blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence “are they taken in their thoughts, which they think.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The sinner hath angered the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one congratulate the man that prospers in his way, to whose sins no avenger is nigh, and an approver is by.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the greater anger of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sinner hath angered the Lord, that he should suffer these things, that is, should not suffer the scourging of correction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The sinner hath angered the Lord: according to the multitude of His anger He will not search it out.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great is His anger, when He searcheth not out, when He as it were forgetteth and marketh not sin, and by fraud and wickedness man attains to riches and honours: which will especially be the case in that Antichrist, who will seem to man blessed to that degree, that he will even be thought God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how great this anger of God is, we are taught by what follows.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God is not in his sight, his ways are polluted in all time” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that knows what in the soul gives joy and gladness, knows how great an ill it is to be abandoned by the light of truth: since a great ill do men reckon the blindness of their bodily eyes, whereby this light is withdrawn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great then the punishment he endures, who through the prosperous issue of his sins is brought to that pass, that God is not in his sight, and that his ways are polluted in all time, that is, his thoughts and counsels are unclean!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy judgments are taken away from his face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the mind conscious of evil, whilst it seems to itself to suffer no punishment, believes that God doth not judge, and so are God’s judgments taken away from its face; while this very thing is great condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And he shall have dominion over all his enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so is it delivered, that he will overcome all kings, and alone obtain the kingdom; since too according to the Apostle, who preaches concerning him, “He shall sit in the temple of God, exalting himself above all that is worshipped and that is called God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And seeing that being delivered over to the lust of his own heart, and predestinated to extreme condemnation, he is to come, by wicked arts, to that vain and empty height and rule; therefore it follows, “For he hath said in his heart, I shall not move from generation to generation without evil” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6): that is, my fame and my name will not pass from this generation to the generation of posterity, unless by evil arts I acquire so lofty a principality, that posterity cannot be silent concerning it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a mind abandoned and void of good arts, and estranged from the light of righteousness, by bad arts devises a passage for itself to a fame so lasting, as is celebrated even in posterity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they that cannot be known for good, desire that men should speak of them even for ill, provided that their name spread far and wide.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this I think is here meant, “I shall not move from generation to generation without evil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is too another interpretation, if a mind vain and full of error supposes that it cannot come from the mortal generation to the generation of eternity, but by bad arts: which indeed was also reported of Simon, when he thought that he would gain heaven by wicked arts, and pass from the human generation to the generation divine by magic.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where then is the wonder, if that man of sin too, who is to fill up all the wickedness and ungodliness, which all false prophets have begun, and to do such “great signs; that, if it were possible, he should deceive the very elect,” shall say in his heart, “I shall not move from generation to generation without evil”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness and deceit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is a great curse to seek heaven by such abominable arts, and to get together such earnings for acquiring the eternal seat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But of this cursing his mouth is full.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this desire shall not take effect, but within his mouth only will avail to destroy him, who dared promise himself such things with bitterness and deceit, that is, with anger and insidiousness, whereby he is to bring over the multitude to his side.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Under his tongue is toil and grief.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing is more toilsome than unrighteousness and ungodliness: upon which toil follows grief; for that the toil is not only without fruit, but even unto destruction.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which toil and grief refer to that which he hath said in his heart, “I shall not be moved from generation to generation without evil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore, “under his tongue,” not on his tongue, because he will devise these things in silence, and to men will speak other things, that he may appear good and just, and a son of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He lieth in ambush with the rich” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What rich, but those whom he will load with this world’s gifts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he is therefore said to lie in ambush with them, because he will display their false happiness to deceive men; who, when with a perverted will they desire to be such as they, and seek not the good things eternal, will fall into his snares.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That in the dark he may kill the innocent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the dark,” I suppose, is said, where it is not easily understood what should be sought, or what avoided.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now to kill the innocent, is of an innocent to make one guilty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His eyes look against the poor,” for he is chiefly to persecute the righteous, of whom it is said, “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He lieth in wait in a secret place, as a lion in his den.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By a lion in a den, he means one in whom both violence and deceit will work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the first persecution of the Church was violent, when by proscriptions, by torments, by murders, the Christians were compelled to sacrifice: another persecution is crafty, which is now conducted by heretics of any kind and false brethren: there remains a third, which is to come by Antichrist, than which there is nothing more perilous; for it will be at once violent and crafty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Violence he will exert in empire, craft in miracles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the violence, the word “lion” refers; to craft, the words “in his den.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And these are again repeated with a change of order.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He lieth in wait,” he says, “that he may catch the poor;” this hath reference to craft: but what follows, “To catch the poor whilst he draweth him,” is put to the score of violence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “draweth” means, he bringeth him to himself by violence, by whatever tortures he can.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, the two which follow are the same.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In his snare he will humble him,” is craft (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall decline and fall, whilst he shall have domination over the poor,” is violence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a “snare” naturally points to “lying in wait:” but domination most openly conveys the idea of terror.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And well does he say, “He will humble him in his snare.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he shall begin to do those signs, the more wonderful they shall appear to men, the more those Saints that shall be then will be despised, and, as it were, set at nought: he, whom they shall resist by righteousness and innocence, shall seem to overcome by the marvels that he does.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “he shall decline and fall, whilst he shall have domination over the poor;” that is, whilst he shall inflict whatsoever punishments he will upon the servants of God that resist him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how shall he decline, and fall?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten; He turneth away His face, that He see not unto the end” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is declining, and the most wretched fall, while the mind of a man prospers as it were in its iniquities, and thinks that it is spared; when it is being blinded, and kept for an extreme and timely vengeance: of which the Psalmist now speaks: “Arise, O Lord God, let Thine hand be exalted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12): that is, let Thy power be made manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now he had said above, “Arise, O Lord, let not man prevail, let the heathen be judged in Thy sight:” that is, in secret, where God alone seeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This comes to pass when the ungodly have arrived at what seems great happiness to men: over whom is placed a lawgiver, such as they had deserved to have, of whom it is said, “Place a lawgiver over them, O Lord, let the heathen know that they are men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But now after that hidden punishment and vengeance it is said, “Arise, O Lord God, let Thine hand be exalted;” not of course in secret, but now in glory most manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That Thou forget not the poor unto the end;” that is, as the ungodly think, who say, “God hath forgotten, He turneth away His face, that He should not see unto the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now they deny that God seeth unto the end, who say that He careth not for things human and earthly, for the earth is as it were the end of things; in that it is the last element, in which men labour in most orderly sort, but they cannot see the order of their labours, which specially belongs to the hidden things of the Son.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church then labouring in such times, like a ship in great waves and tempests, awaketh the Lord as if He were sleeping, that He should command the winds, and calm should be restored.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He says therefore, “Arise, O Lord God, let Thine hand be exalted, that Thou forget not the poor unto the end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly understanding now the manifest judgment, and in exultation at it, they say, “Wherefore hath the ungodly angered God?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 13); that is, what hath it profited him to do so great evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he said in his heart, He will not require it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then follows, “For Thou seest toil and considerest anger, to deliver them into Thine hands” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This sentence looks for distinct explanation, wherein if there shall be error it becomes obscure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thus has the ungodly said in his heart, God will not require it, as though God regarded toil and anger, to deliver them into His hands; that is, as though He feared toil and anger, and for this reason would spare them, lest their punishment be too burdensome to Him, or lest He should be disturbed by the storm of anger: as men generally act, excusing themselves of vengeance, to avoid toil or anger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The poor hath been left unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For therefore is he poor, that is, hath despised all the temporal goods of this world, that Thou only mayest be his hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou wilt be a helper to the orphan,” that is, to him to whom his father this world, by whom he was born after the flesh, dies, and who can already say, “The world hath been crucified unto me, and I unto the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of such orphans God becomes the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord teaches us in truth that His disciples do become orphans, to whom He saith, “Call no man father on earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of which He first Himself gave an example in saying, “Who is my mother, and who my brethren?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence some most mischievous heretics would assert that He had no mother; and they do not see that it follows from this, if they pay attention to these words, that neither had His disciples fathers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as He said, “Who is my mother?” so He taught them, when He said, “Call no man your father on earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Break the arm of the sinner and of the malicious” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 15); of him, namely, of whom it was said above, “He shall have dominion over all his enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He called his power then, his arm; to which Christ’s power is opposed, of which it is said, “Arise, O Lord God, let Thine hand be exalted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His fault shall be required, and he shall not be found because of it;” that is he shall be judged for his sins, and himself shall perish because of his sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After this, what wonder if there follow, “The Lord shall reign for ever and world without end; ye heathen shall perish out of His earth”? (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He uses heathen for sinners and ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord hath heard the longing of the poor” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 17): that longing wherewith they were burning, when in the straits and tribulations of this world they desired the day of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thine ear hath heard the preparation of their heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the preparation of the heart, of which it is sung in another Psalm, “My heart is prepared, O God, my heart is prepared:” of which the Apostle says, “But if we hope for what we see not, we do with patience wait for it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, by the ear of God, we ought, according to a general rule of interpretation, to understand not a bodily member, but the power whereby He heareth; and so (not to repeat this often) by whatever members of His are mentioned, which in us are visible and bodily, must be understood powers of operation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we must not suppose it anything bodily, in that the Lord God hears not the sound of the voice, but the preparation of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To judge for the orphan and the humble” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 18): that is, not for him who is conformed to this world, nor for the proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is one thing to judge the orphan, another to judge for the orphan.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He judges the orphan even, who condemns him; but he judges for the orphan, who delivers sentence for him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That man add not further to magnify himself upon earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are men, of whom it was said, “Place a lawgiver over them, O Lord: let the heathen know that they are men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he too, who in this same passage is understood to be placed over them, will be man, of whom it is now said, “That man add not further to magnify himself upon earth:” namely, when the Son of Man shall come to judge for the orphan, who hath put off from himself the old man, and thus, as it were, buried his father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After the hidden things then of the Son, of which, in this Psalm, many things have been said, will come the manifest things of the Son, of which a little has been now said at the end of the same Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the title is given from the former, which here occupy the larger portion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Indeed, the very day of the Lord’s advent may be rightly numbered among the hidden things of the Son, although the very presence of the Lord itself will be manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of that day it is said, that no man knoweth it, neither angels, nor powers, nor the Son of man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then so hidden, as that which is said to be hidden even to the Judge Himself, not as regards knowledge, but disclosure?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But concerning the hidden things of the Son, even if any one would not wish to understand the Son of God, but of David himself, to whose name the whole Psalter is attributed, for the Psalms we know are called the Psalms of David, let him give ear to those words in which it is said to the Lord, “Have mercy on us, O Son of David:” and so even in this manner let him understand the same Lord Christ, concerning whose hidden things is the inscription of this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so likewise is it said by the Angel: “God shall give unto Him the throne of His father David.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor to this understanding of it is the sentence opposed in which the same Lord asks of the Jews, “If Christ be the Son of David, how then doth he in spirit call Him Lord, saying, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou on my right hand, until I put Thine enemies under Thy feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was said to the unskilled, who although they looked for Christ’s coming, yet expected Him as man, not as the Power and Wisdom of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He teacheth then, in that place, the most true and pure faith, that He is both the Lord of king David, in that He is the Word in the beginning, God with God, by which all things were made; and Son, in that He was made to him of the seed of David according to the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He doth not say, Christ is not David’s Son, but if ye already hold that He is his Son, learn how He is his Lord: and do not hold in respect of Christ that He is the Son of Man, for so is He David’s Son; and leave out that He is the Son of God, for so is He David’s Lord.
